This family is part of the larger order Asterales, which includes other flowering plants such as sunflowers and daisies. Velleia pilosella is an endemic species found in Australia.

intro: Velleia pilosella, also known as the hairy blue velleia, is a small, perennial herb native to Australia, belonging to the family Goodeniaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. Velleia pilosella contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal medicine and is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a popular ingredient in traditional Australian remedies.
